avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions. The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on north facing slopes, as well as on steep east, south and west facing slopes between approximately 2500 and 3100 m.

In particular on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover show the existence of a weak snowack.
Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate easterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed on Monday. The new snow of Monday has settled to a large extent.

Below approximately 3000 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an appreciable increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Natural avalanches are possible in the afternoon. Avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released, even by small loads in isolated cases, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m.
Steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are generally favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Some rain will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2900 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. On steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2900 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: On Sunday it will be partly cloudy. Above approximately 2600 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in the afternoon.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are generally favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Some rain will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.
Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover show the existence of a weak snowack.

In the afternoon the weather will be partly cloudy in some regions. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Monday above approximately 2300 m. The new snow of Monday has settled to a large extent.

Below approximately 3000 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack. The high temperatures in the late morning will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack on sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As the moisture increases the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover and thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude, and are steadily decreasing. Weather conditions will mean the snowpack will remain saturated in many areas. Over a wide area a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Over a wide area rain below approximately 2000 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of warming and solar radiation a sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for moist avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2800 m. Bases of rock walls are especially dangerous. From starting zones that have retained some snow on very steep shady slopes moist avalanches can in very isolated cases reach large size. As a consequence of wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed especially above approximately 2600 m. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous.
Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2700 m. The new snow of Tuesday is loosely bonded above approximately 2800 m. The wind slabs are poorly bonded with the old snowpack above approximately 2700 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place gliding avalanches can reach intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. Great caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and rain up to high altitudes an unfavourable avalanche situation developed during the last two days. The snowpack is wet. Gliding avalanches can be released at any time of day or night.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions over a wide area, but the danger of wet avalanches will increase later.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
In particular on steep sunny slopes small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the existence of a weak snowack.

Backcountry tours should be concluded early. In the afternoon the weather will be partly cloudy in some regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 5 to 30 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2100 m.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes below approximately 2600 m, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be mostly sunny over a wide area.
The weather will be warm.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.
As a consequence of wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
From the middle of the day the natural activity of avalanches will increase. In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are possible.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Monday above approximately 2300 m. The weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. As the moisture increases more moist and wet avalanches are possible.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a gradual increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
As a consequence of the snow drift accumulations will stabilise during the next few days.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. More moist and wet avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: On Sunday it will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be mostly sunny over a wide area.
The weather will be warm.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
